entry.title: Posted by: Sarah
entry.summary: I love how you were able to capture the movement of the water, it really does look like it is still flowing. I think it is just amazing!
entry.content: I love how you were able to capture the movement of the water, it really does look like it is still flowing. I think it is just amazing!

entry.title: Posted by: Wm. Minchin
entry.summary: Bridget, see if you camera has a 'Tv' setting (for “Time Value”).
entry.content: Bridget, see if you camera has a 'Tv' setting (for “Time Value”).

entry.title: Posted by: Bridget
entry.summary: I love this Will. I tried to take a similar picture of a waterfall and could not figure it out! Now that I know that a tenth of a second is the right shutter speed, I just need to figure out how to change the shutter speed on my camera …
entry.content: I love this Will. I tried to take a similar picture of a waterfall and could not figure it out! Now that I know that a tenth of a second is the right shutter speed, I just need to figure out how to change the shutter speed on my camera… : ) I love looking at your photos.

entry.title: Posted by: randy
entry.summary:
Hi, Nice post thanks for sharing. Would you please consider adding a link to my website on your page. Please email me back.

Thanks!

Randy
randydavis387@gmail.com
entry.content:
Hi, Nice post thanks for sharing. Would you please consider adding a link to my website on your page. Please email me back.
